headline: BofA Reinstates Coverage of ServiceNow, Salesforce. It Says 1 Is an AI Beneficiary.
summary: Bank of America analysts reinstated coverage of two software plays on Monday, but took widely different views about how they will be affected by artificial intelligence.  A team led by Tal Liani reinstated coverage of  ServiceNow  with a Buy rating and a $130 price target, which currently represents 28% upside.  Meanwhile, the firm reinstated coverage of  Salesforce  with an Underperform rating and a $160 price target.

headline: BofA Reinstates ServiceNow at Buy: The Workflow Giant Built for the Agentic AI Era
summary: Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) reinstated its coverage of ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) with a Buy rating and a $130 price target on May 18. The call frames the workflow software leader as a clear AI beneficiary, even as Bank of America reinstated Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) at Underperform on the same day. headline: One Lane Closed. The Whole Rate Picture Changed
summary: One shipping lane controls roughly 20 percent of the world's oil supply. Right now, it's closed. And in the space of five trading days, that single fact has moved oil to $105 a barrel, pushed Treasury yields to their highest point in roughly a year, and resurrected a rate hike scenario that nobody was pricing a week ago. Welcome to Monday.

Welcome to Monday.

headline: Is It Time To Reconsider Salesforce (CRM) After This Year’s 32% Share Price Slide?
summary: If you are wondering whether Salesforce at around US$173.51 is starting to look attractive or still feels expensive, the next sections will walk through the numbers in plain language. The stock has been under pressure recently, with the share price down 4.6% over the last week, 4.7% over the last month and 31.6% year to date, contributing to a 40.0% decline over the past year. These moves sit against a backdrop of continued attention on large software platforms and how they fit into broader...
